reasoning: The threshold to beat is a 360-day average of about 3.167 protests per 30 days, meaning at least 4 protests must take place in the 30-day windows prior to each date (Oct-Nov 2025, Nov 2025, and Dec 2025-Jan 2026). Multiple searches specifically targeting Martinique protests and news from late 2025 and January 2026 returned no relevant results about ongoing unrest. The massive cost-of-living protests that swept the island in late 2024 and early 2025 largely subsided after March 2025, when significant government concessions on grocery prices and other issues were secured. Considering that major demonstrations have historically attracted international news—even from local Caribbean outlets—the absence of news strongly implies that there were fewer than 4 protests in the targeted 30-day windows. Therefore, resolution to YES is highly unlikely for all dates, with uncertainty properly scaled across the forecast horizons.
